Panama / Coast

Black sand, bright fruit, old questions

May 17, 2026 6 min read

What a volcanic beach taught me about choosing fewer things and feeling them all the way through.

The beach was dark enough to look lunar. Black sand stuck to my ankles while the fruit vendor cut mango with the kind of calm precision that makes you feel overdressed in your own urgency.

I bought two pieces and carried them to the waterline. The ocean was loud. The town behind me was louder. Somewhere between them, an old question showed up again: how much of my life is actually chosen?

Travel has a way of making that question less theoretical. You feel every extra thing you packed. You notice every reflex to fill silence. You learn quickly which comforts are useful and which ones are just weight with a nicer name.

The mango was bright, almost electric against the sand. I ate it slowly and tried to let that be the whole assignment.

Now Roaming you are now roaming Travel / Experience / Enlightenment
